title: Share of adults with legal documentation of their rights to land
subtitle: The proportion of the total adult population with secure tenure rights to land, recognized with legal documentation.
caption:
Share of adults with legal documentation of their rights to land

The proportion of the total adult population with secure tenure rights to land, recognized with legal documentation.

This horizontal bar chart displays the share of adults with legal documentation of their rights to land for six specific countries, with data points ranging from 2018 to 2021. Rwanda occupies the top position with a significantly longer bar than the others, showing a value of 41.15% based on 2019 data. Following Rwanda, Benin and Togo present similar bar lengths in the mid-range of the chart; Benin is recorded at 12.5% in 2021, while Togo is slightly lower at 11.82% in the same year. Pakistan appears next with a value of 9.84% from 2018. The lower section of the chart features Tanzania at 6.61%, with no specific year listed next to its value. Malawi sits at the bottom with the shortest bar, representing 0.63% in 2019. The visual arrangement highlights a substantial gap between the top entry and the remaining countries, with the values for Benin, Togo, and Pakistan clustering between roughly 10% and 12.5%, while Tanzania and Malawi fall below 7%. The bars are arranged in descending order of magnitude, clearly distinguishing the highest proportion in Rwanda from the single-digit percentages observed in the other five nations.
